title: The Broach and Kaira Incumbered Estates Act, 1877
full_text: The Broach and Kaira Incumbered Estates Act, 1877 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 187714 Act Number: 14 Enactment Date: 1877-06-28 Act Year: 1877 Short Title: The Broach and Kaira Incumbered Estates Act, 1877 Long Title: An Act to relieve from Incumbrances the estates of Thakurs in Broach and Kaira. Ministry: Ministry of Home Affairs Department: Department of States Enforcement Date: 28-06-1877 TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Broach and Kaira Incumbered Estates Act, 1877 I.—PRELIMINARY II.—OF THE APPLICATION AND PRELIMINARY INQUIRY. III.—OF THE ORDER OF MANAGEMENT IV.—PROOF OF DEBTS AND SCHEME FOR LIQUIDATION. V.—OF THE PROCEEDINGS SUBSEQUENT TO SANCTION OF THE LIQUIDATION-SCHEME. VI.—OF APPEAL AND REVISION. VII.—MISCELLANEOUS. Section 1. Short title. Section 2. Act No. XV of 1871 repealed. Section 3. Interpretation-clause. Section 4. Application for benefit of Act. Section 5. Order to enquire. Section 6. Verified statement to be submitted. Section 7. Report of enquiry and proceedings thereon. Section 8. “Order of management” to what it extends. Section 9. Effect of order of management. Section 10. Manager to have powers of owner and to receive rents and profits: to have powers of Collector for their recovery. Section 11. Manager to pay therefrom-- costs of management and repairs, Government revenue, &c., rent due to superior holder, allowance for maintenance and expenses of debtor and family, cost of improvements, &c. Residue how disposed of. Section 12. Notice to claimants against debtor. Section 13. Claim to contain full particulars. Section 14. Claim not duly notified to be barred. Section 15. Determination of debts and liabilities. Section 16. Power to rank debts and to fix interest. Section 17. Scheme for liquidation. Section 18. Proceedings of Commissioner on submission of scheme. Section 19. Power to relinquish management. Section 20. Effects of sanctioning scheme. Section 21. Power to remove mortgagee in possession. Section 22. Power to inquire into consideration given for leases. Section 23. Power to lease. Section 24. Power to raise money by mortgage or sale. Section 25. Manager’s receipt a discharge. Section 26. Termination of management. Section 27. Death of debtor during management. Section 28. Mortgages, &c., made by restored Thakur valid only for his life. Section 29. Appeal. Section 30. Power to call for proceedings and pass order thereon. Section VII. MISCELLANEOUS. Section 31. Power to make rules. Section 32. Power to appoint new manager. Section 33. Managers and their agents to be public servants. Section 34. Investigation, a judicial proceeding. Section 35. Power to summon witnesses and compel production of documents. Section 36. Bar of suits. Section 37. Saving of jurisdiction of Courts in Broach and Kaira in respect of certain suits. Section 38. Exemption of certain Thakurs from certain provisions of Act. Section 39. Amendment of Bombay Act VI of 1862. Section 41. Acts of Taluqdari Settlement officer valid.
